content: A three-stage progressive reinforcement learning framework can be repurposed to structure the curation of an active learning agent's episodic memory, where each stage progressively prunes, generalizes, and prioritizes stored memories based on intrinsic curiosity signals rather than just task reward. This structured memory curation resolves the common problem of unconstrained memory growth that plagues state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents by aligning memory retention with the agent's intrinsic drive to explore unknown state spaces.
